purpose: Verify the lsrs_by_point endpoint serves ARBITRARY PAST single-calendar-day windows for a 'what hit this point on this date' tool, BEFORE writing code.

endpoint.validation_stack: FastAPI + Pydantic (structured HTTP 422 JSON {'detail':[...]} on bad/missing params)

params.lon: REQUIRED, decimal deg E. Missing -> HTTP 422 {'type':'missing','loc':['query','lon']}
params.lat: REQUIRED, decimal deg N. Missing -> HTTP 422.
params.radius_miles: 0 < radius_miles < 1000 STRICTLY. 999=OK(200), 1000=HTTP 422 'Input should be less than 1000', 1001/5000=422. Mutually-usable with radius_degrees.
params.begints: UTC ISO datetime. Tolerant parser: 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ', WITHOUT trailing Z, and '+00:00' offset ALL accepted and ALL treated as UTC (identical 43-feature result). Malformed value -> HTTP 422 'datetime_from_date_parsing'.
params.endts: UTC ISO datetime, same parser as begints.

CRITICAL_both_time_params_required.claim: BOTH begints AND endts required or the time filter is SILENTLY IGNORED.

evidence_2024-05-07_r50_OKC.begints_only: HTTP 200, 6961 features (ENTIRE all-time history at point - filter ignored, NO error)

CRITICAL_both_time_params_required.implementer_warning: A missing/empty begints OR endts does NOT 422 - it returns the full multi-thousand-feature point history with HTTP 200. The tool MUST always send both, and should sanity-check that returned 'valid' timestamps fall inside the requested window.

single_day_query_used_for_fixture.result: HTTP 200, 43 features, 22610 bytes raw; 0 features outside requested UTC day
single_day_query_used_for_fixture.why_chosen: Found by scanning wide window 2024-04-01..2024-06-15 (465 feats); 2024-05-07 was the busiest at 100mi (69 feats incl 9 tornadoes). At radius 50 it has 3 tornadoes + hail + wind + damage + flash flood = good multi-type populated single day.

second_day_confirmation.result: HTTP 200, 23 features (all type N = NON-TSTM WND GST), proves repeatability for a different past year/season

data_floor_and_quality_cliff.endpoint_floor_at_this_OKC_point: 2005-06-09T15:40:00Z is the OLDEST record returned by an all-history pull (radius_miles=50, no time filter, 6961 feats).
data_floor_and_quality_cliff.pre_2005_returns_EMPTY_not_error: Explicit windows 2002 May-Jun, 2003 May-Jun, 2004 May-Jun, AND 2005 May ALL return HTTP 200 with 0 features. So the realistic floor here is mid-2005, NOT 2002 as the legacy lsrs-table note claims. The /api/1/nws/lsrs_by_point service appears to query a narrower slice than the raw lsrs table.
data_floor_and_quality_cliff.implementer_warning: Do NOT advertise pre-2005 coverage for this endpoint. An empty result for an old date is indistinguishable (same HTTP 200, empty FeatureCollection) from 'no reports that day' - cannot tell 'before data existed' from 'quiet day' from the response alone.

row_limit_behavior.NO_observable_cap: Unlike the legacy /geojson/lsr.geojson (LIMIT 10000), lsrs_by_point returned 37663 features (radius 500mi, full year 2024) and 300451 features (radius 999mi, 2 years 2023-2024), BOTH HTTP 200. No truncation field, no 413, no cap hit.
row_limit_behavior.implementer_warning: A wide radius + long window can return HUNDREDS OF THOUSANDS of features = tens of MB JSON. For a single-day point tool keep radius_miles small (<=50) and window to one calendar day; otherwise add client-side guardrails. There is NO server-side safety limit to rely on.

per_feature_schema_HISTORICAL.name: pyogrio_<hex> (volatile per-request; do not depend on)

properties.type: str 1-char LSR class code (T/H/G/D/N/F/R/E/... same code table as live)
properties.typetext: str uppercase display text e.g. 'TORNADO','HAIL','TSTM WND GST','TSTM WND DMG'
properties.magnitude: NUMBER (float) or null. null for T/D/F (no magnitude); float for H(inch)/G(mph)/R(inch). NOTE: this endpoint returns magnitude as a NUMBER, not the legacy string. No 0.0 sentinel observed.
properties.unit: str or null. 'Inch' (hail/rain), 'MPH' (wind). null when no magnitude.
properties.qualifier: str or null. 'M'=measured, 'E'=estimated. null when no magnitude.
properties.city: str location, may be relative e.g. '8 S Omega','4 SSE Carney'
properties.product_id: str e.g. '202405070244-KOUN-NWUS54-LSROUN'. WARNING: product_id timestamp prefix can be DAYS AFTER the event 'valid' time (delayed LSR issuance) - e.g. an event valid 2024-05-07T03:58Z had product_id '202405100346-...'. Do NOT derive event date from product_id; use 'valid'.

per_feature_schema_HISTORICAL.ALL_17_props_present_on_every_feature: 43/43 features carry all 17 keys (no per-feature key omission)

data_quality_gotchas: CORRECTED reports can be internally inconsistent: caught a feature with type='R' (RAIN) carrying magnitude=59.0 unit='Inch' qualifier='M' whose remark was 'Corrects previous rain report... Its wind, not rain.' - type/unit/magnitude were NOT all updated together. Trust 'type' for classification but be aware unit/magnitude can be stale on corrections.
mPING / crowd-sourced reports appear with source='Public' and remark 'Report from mPING; time is estimated based on radar.' - 'valid' is radar-estimated, not observed.
product_id issuance can lag the event by days (see schema note) - never compute the storm date from product_id.

fixtures_saved.iem_lsr_historical_sample.geojson: RAW untrimmed 43-feature response from lon=-97.52 lat=35.47 radius_miles=50 begints=2024-05-07T00:00:00Z endts=2024-05-07T23:59:59Z. Real recorded response, indent=1 reformatted only (feature contents byte-faithful).
